author: us-equity-research full-round orchestrator (Phases 0-3 + Verification)

headline.headline_text: Source-conditional SELL / Underweight at $146.9 (S4): probability-weighted 12-month expected return -14.2% to a base-case PT of $124.94 - an INDEPENDENT fair value (DCF $107 / SOTP $132 / de-rated comps -> ~$125, -15% vs spot), NOT the spot-anchored 14x that produced the prior near-zero Hold. The v1 Hold held VST's ~14x forward P/E (a peak AI-power multiple); de-anchored to the through-cycle / private-market band (~11x, where VST itself bought Cogentrix at ~7.25x EV/EBITDA), fair value is ~15% below spot. We are far below the +50%-ish Street PT. CONDITIONAL on the ~35% open 2028 book repricing toward EIA base and PJM capacity reverting off the FERC cap; cover toward Hold on a sub-$120 entry or a >$300 PJM 2028/29 BRA print. Sized Sell/Underweight (not Strong Sell) given the AI-power-demand timing tail; cleanest expression is the short leg of long-CEG... no, the relative short vs the cohort.

recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: DE-BIASED Sell/Underweight on VST at $146.9. Methodological rebuild of the prior Hold: anchoring the base to an INDEPENDENT fair value (DCF $107, SOTP $132, comps de-rated to ~$130 -> weighted ~$125, ~15% below spot) instead of holding VST's elevated ~14x forward P/E on peak AI-power EBITDA. VST's own capital allocation marks private fair value below the public multiple (Cogentrix ~7.25x EV/EBITDA), and the ~35% open 2028 book + PJM-capacity-at-the-cap make the current multiple a peak-on-peak. Base PT $124.94 = FY27E EPS $10.96 x P/E 11.4x (through-cycle/private-market band). Load-bearing variance V1 (2028 open-book de-rate) sized 2.16pp with S1 evidence; our PT-implied return is well below the consensus PT (~$230). Probability-weighted -14.2% (base -14.9%); strong_bear $69.7 / strong_bull $217.6. The AI-power-demand + PPA-conversion timing tail is real (deficits/scarcity are sticky), so this is sized Sell/Underweight + a relative short within the IPP sleeve, not a Strong Sell or aggressive outright short. Cover: sub-$120 or a >$300 PJM 2028/29 BRA.

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Cheapest AI-power IPP with the integrated-retail hedge
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~9.4x 2026E EV/EBITDA & ~7.2% FCF yield vs CEG ~11.4x / ~3.8% (S4: peer comps 2026-06)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: VST is the value leg of the merchant-power/AI-power cohort. EV/kW ~$1,360 is the lowest among CEG/NRG/TLN. The structural differentiator vs pure-nuclear CEG is the ~5M-customer competitive retail book, which naturally hedges generation (~100% of 2026 generation hedged) and smooths merchant volatility. The discount to CEG (~2 turns) is partly justified (nuclear-purity + commodity beta) but ~0.5-1.0 turn appears wider than fundamentals warrant post-Calpine, when CEG took on its own integration + FERC divestiture.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Re-rating contingent on capacity durability + PPA conversion through 2027

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Contracted de-risking via 20-yr investment-grade hyperscaler PPAs
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: 3,809 MW signed 20-yr nuclear PPAs (AWS 1,200 MW + Meta 2,609 MW), ~$110/MWh ~2x ERCOT merchant (S2: 8-Ks; S5 pricing est.)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: AWS (Comanche Peak, ERCOT, Q4'27 deliveries) and Meta (PJM nuclear + uprates) convert ~9% of fleet MW into multi-decade IG annuities at ~2x merchant pricing, ~8-10% incremental FCFbG accretion at full AWS utilization. AWS is front-of-meter in ERCOT (outside FERC RTO co-location jurisdiction), sidestepping the Talen/Susquehanna behind-the-meter problem. The next leg (Comanche Unit 2 uncontracted ~1,200 MW; gas co-location) is real optionality but un-signed as of mid-2026.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: AWS deliveries Q4 2027; next PPA conversion is the bull swing variable

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Capacity-market tailwind + balance-sheet de-risking to crossover IG
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: PJM 2027/28 cleared at FERC cap $333.44/MW-day UCAP (~$1.3B to VST); IG at S&P (BBB-, Dec'25) + Fitch (BBB-, Mar'26) (S1/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: PJM capacity cleared at the FERC offer cap for two consecutive auctions (~40-63% data-center-driven), and VST reached investment grade at 2 of 3 agencies in Q1'26, which also triggered collateral release on senior secured notes. Net leverage 2.78x FY25 (calculated; not company-reported) vs a <3.0x target. ~$1.5B buyback remaining retires ~3.5-4%/yr of shares net of trivial SBC.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: PJM cap/collar binds through 2029/30; real capacity cliff is post-2029/30 expiry

investment_thesis_pillars.title: The bear: commodity cyclical priced near peak EBITDA on a peak multiple
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Private-market gas M&A ~7-7.5x EV/EBITDA (VST paid ~7.25x for Cogentrix) vs public ~9.4x; ~35% of 2028 generation open (S1: hedge ratios)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: VST's own capital allocation (Cogentrix at ~7.25x) and NRG/LS Power deals (~7.5x) mark private fair value below the public multiple, and that gap closes DOWN when the AI narrative wobbles. PJM capacity upside is regulation-truncated (cap) with downside to a $179.55 floor; EIA modeling shows PJM power upside ~+4% even in a high-demand scenario; the 45U nuclear PTC (~$545M, ~9% of EBITDA) self-erases at high power prices and sunsets end-2031/2032. The de-rate mechanism (private<public, multiple + EBITDA both near cycle peak) is intact even though the trigger probability fell this quarter.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Bear confirms on a sub-$250 PJM 2028/29 BRA print (~July 2026)

company_qualitative.description: Vistra Corp. (VST) is the largest US competitive (non-regulated, merchant) integrated electricity and power-generation company, operating a ~41 GW diversified fleet (natural gas, nuclear, coal, solar, battery storage) vertically integrated with a leading competitive retail electricity business (~5 million customers; TXU Energy, Dynegy, Ambit and other brands). It operates in deregulated markets: ERCOT (Texas), PJM (East/Mid-Atlantic), and ISO-NE/NYISO/CAISO (West). Reporting segments: Retail; Texas; East; West; Sunset/Asset Closure; Corporate. FY25 revenue was $17,738M (S1: VST FY25 10-K, XBRL companyfacts CIK 0001692819) with GAAP net income $944M and Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA $5,912M. GAAP net income is non-informational for this business: it is dominated by ASC 815 unrealized derivative mark-to-market on the commodity hedge book (FY25 carried an ~$808M unrealized hedge loss; Q1'26 an ~$723M unrealized gain), so GAAP NI swung $1,493M -> $2,659M -> $944M FY23-25 while Adjusted EBITDA rose steadily. The 2024-2026 narrative is AI/data-center power demand: VST signed 20-year PPAs with AWS (1,200 MW carbon-free from Comanche Peak nuclear in ERCOT; deliveries Q4 2027 ramping to full by 2032) and Meta (>2,600 MW of energy/capacity/uprates across its PJM nuclear fleet: Beaver Valley PA, Perry & Davis-Besse OH). The fleet was scaled by the 2024 Energy Harbor nuclear acquisition (~4 GW), the Lotus 2.6 GW gas acquisition (closed Nov 2025), and the pending $4B Cogentrix 5,496 MW gas acquisition (announced Jan 5 2026; expected close mid-to-late 2026). ~4.6 GW of coal faces EPA-driven retirement in 2027-2028.

products.name: Generation - Nuclear (~6.4 GW)
products.description: Comanche Peak (TX ~2.4 GW), Beaver Valley (PA), Davis-Besse (OH), Perry (OH). Carbon-free baseload anchoring the AWS + Meta PPAs; earns the 45U production tax credit (~$545M).
products.target_customers: Wholesale ISO markets + AWS/Meta long-term PPAs
products.competitive_position: Second-largest US competitive nuclear fleet behind Constellation

products.name: Generation - Natural gas
products.description: Large CCGT + peaker fleet across ERCOT/PJM; +2.6 GW Lotus (closed Nov 2025) and +5.5 GW Cogentrix (pending). Dispatchable capacity earning PJM capacity revenue at the FERC cap.
products.target_customers: ISO energy + capacity markets; gas co-location pipeline
products.competitive_position: Largest US merchant gas fleet post-Cogentrix

products.name: Generation - Coal (retiring) + solar/storage
products.description: ~4.6 GW coal retiring 2027-2028 (EPA); growing solar + battery storage. Asset Closure segment carries decommissioning/ARO.
products.competitive_position: Decarbonizing; coal cliff offsets organic adds

products.name: Retail electricity (~5M customers)
products.description: Competitive retail (TXU Energy, Dynegy, Ambit, etc.) acting as the integrated hedge for generation; record FY25 EBITDA $1,622M guided to normalize ~$1.4B.
products.pricing_model: Retail margin over hedged supply cost
products.competitive_position: Top-tier competitive retailer; integration smooths merchant volatility

company_qualitative.customers_gtm: Generation sells energy + capacity into ISO/RTO markets (ERCOT energy-only; PJM/ISO-NE/NYISO capacity + energy) plus long-term IG-counterparty PPAs (AWS, Meta). Retail sells to ~5M residential/commercial customers, internally hedging generation (~100% of 2026 generation covered). Counterparty concentration in the PPA book skews to Meta (~69%) and AWS (~31%), both top-decile investment grade.

industry.overview: US power demand is growing at its fastest rate since 2000, driven by data-center/AI load, with EIA/FERC load-growth forecasts revised up repeatedly. Merchant IPPs (Constellation, Vistra, NRG, Talen, Calpine-now-CEG) are mid-to-late-cycle in an AI-power upcycle: PJM capacity has cleared at the FERC offer cap for two consecutive auctions and hyperscalers have procured multiple GW of nuclear. Gas-turbine supply is sold out to ~2028-2030 and interconnection queues are multi-year, delaying the supply response. The bear nuance (EIA modeling) is that power-price upside is regionally narrow: ERCOT-North spikes only in a high-demand scenario and PJM moves only ~+4% in the same scenario. The Constellation-Calpine merger closed Jan 7 2026 (~60 GW; CEG is now the largest US private-sector producer), with a ~4.4 GW PJM divestiture to LS Power (~$5B, announced Mar 2026).

source.ref: EIA STEO + load-growth forecasts; US wholesale power + capacity + retail dollarized

peers.name: Constellation Energy
peers.key_differentiator: Largest US nuclear fleet (~32 GW) + Calpine gas (~26 GW); MSFT Crane/TMI restart; cleaner balance sheet (~1.7x leverage)

peers.name: NRG Energy
peers.key_differentiator: Largest competitive retail base (~6M + Vivint); ~76% gas; minimal nuclear; LS Power gas added

competitive_landscape.positioning_narrative: VST is the value/integrated leg of the AI-power cohort: cheapest on EV/EBITDA and EV/kW, highest FCF yield, and uniquely hedged by a ~5M-customer retail book. CEG is the quality/nuclear-purity leg (lower leverage, MSFT halo, scarcest 24/7 carbon-free asset) and commands a ~2-turn premium that is ~1.0-1.3 turns defensible post-Calpine. NRG is retail-heavy/gas with minimal nuclear; TLN is a nuclear-heavy small-cap. On a 12-month horizon the dominant risk is power/capacity-price realization on the ~35% open 2028 book and the hedge-collateral liquidity tail; on a multi-year horizon it is whether AI/data-center demand sustains the capacity-price and PPA-pricing regime that current EBITDA growth assumes.

management.name: Jim Burke
management.title: President & CEO
management.bio: President & CEO of Vistra (CEO since 2023; previously COO and CFO). Long Vistra/TXU tenure; architect of the integrated retail-generation hedge model and the AI-power PPA strategy (AWS, Meta) and the Energy Harbor/Lotus/Cogentrix acquisition program.

management.name: Kristopher Moldovan
management.title: EVP & CFO
management.bio: CFO responsible for the capital-allocation framework (~$6.3B buybacks since Nov 2021; ~$1.5B remaining), the investment-grade balance-sheet transition (S&P + Fitch BBB- in Q1'26), and Cogentrix financing.

source.ref: Modeled energy-margin allocation: retail revenue gross, contribution-margin proxy (VST does not disclose segment GM; IPP GM analog per gm-taxonomy IPP adaptation)
Modeled: ERCOT generation (Comanche Peak + gas) energy-margin proxy
Modeled: PJM nuclear + capacity + Meta PPA energy-margin proxy (highest-margin segment)

entries.name: Consolidated energy gross margin LTM-Q1FY26 (IPP analog: revenue less fuel + purchased power + direct operating cost)

source.ref: Modeled LTM weighted reconciliation; VST reports segment Adjusted EBITDA, not GM (IPP gm-taxonomy adaptation, analogous to NIM for banks)

entries.name: Segment energy margin: East (PJM nuclear + capacity + Meta) highest at ~42%; Texas ~35%; Retail ~20%; West ~15%

source.ref: Modeled segment energy-margin allocation; reconciles to consolidated within rounding

entries.name: Nuclear sub-segment margin (contracted AWS/Meta PPAs at ~$110/MWh ~2x merchant) vs open merchant generation

source.ref: Modeled: PPA pricing ~2x ERCOT merchant (S5 estimate); contracted slice higher margin

entries.name: FY27E blended generation margin with Cogentrix + Meta uprates in the mix

source.ref: Consensus FY27E EBITDA margin proxy n=17

entries.name: Marginal open-generation margin sensitivity to power price (the load-bearing 2028 lever)
entries.value_range: At PJM cap + EIA-base power: ~33%; at capacity-floor + open book -15-20%: ~26-28% (2028 EBITDA swing -$0.6 to -$0.9B base / -$1.4 to -$2.3B tail)

source.ref: VST hedge ratios 98/89/65% 2026/27/28 (S1) + EIA STEO power-price scenarios (S5)

methods.narrative: DE-BIASED: de-rated to ~8.0x 2027E EBITDA (toward the private-market gas band ~7.25-7.5x VST/NRG paid), not the spot-anchored 8.75x -> ~$130. The v1 8.75x reached ~$153 = the spot-anchored base.

input_attribution.verifiable: the earnings/DCF-per-unit base (buildable bottom-up; tends to converge)
input_attribution.assumed: the multiple — the contested judgment where the bull/bear gap lives

methods.narrative: 10y FCFbG DCF; WACC 9.61% (CoE 11.24% at beta 1.41 + after-tax Kd 4.74%); g 2.25%. Base ~$107, below spot - disciplines the merchant-cyclical tail and reflects that the market price embeds AI-power/PPA-conversion optionality a contracted DCF cannot underwrite.

Sum-of-parts: East (PJM nuclear + capacity + Meta) at 9.5x, Texas (Comanche + AWS) 8.5x, Retail 7.0x, West 6.0x, less coal/closure drag and net debt. Monotonicity NI<=OP<=GP<=Revenue holds per G3. ~$132/share, between DCF and comps.

Bear floor reference: 2028 EBITDA reverting to ~$5.5B (capacity toward floor + open-book reprice -15-20% + retail normalization + PTC fade) x 7.5x private-market multiple ~ $73-74; severe (margin event) ~$53. Not weighted; downside discipline that anchors strong_bear $73.80.

valuation.reconciliation_narrative: DE-BIASED reconcile: DCF $107, SOTP $132, de-rated comps $130 -> independent weighted fair value ~$125, ~15% BELOW spot $146.9. The 5-scenario base PT $124.94 (FY27E EPS $10.96 x 11.4x) is anchored to that fair value, not to the elevated ~14x. The only change vs the v1 Hold: the base no longer holds the peak AI-power multiple - it de-rates to the through-cycle/private-market band, which is why fair value is below spot and the rating is Sell/Underweight.

components.rationale: Base EPS/DCF-per-unit unchanged — the de-bias changed NO estimates (base_eps held fixed so bridges reconcile); public inputs identical before/after.
Re-anchored the base from the consensus-PT/current-multiple regime to an independent fair-value blend — the de-bias itself.
Re-rated the base multiple toward the through-cycle / private-market band consistent with independent fair value (~$125).

revision_bridge.judgment_flag: 100%% of this revision is methodology + multiple (judgment), 0%% new estimates — by design a deliberate anchor CORRECTION (de-bias), not new information; flagged per Lesson 2. Public inputs only (consensus PT, SEC/XBRL); any entitlement-restricted cross-check is a personal, non-persisted localized optimization outside the framework.

valuation.load_bearing_input_note: The disagreement lives in the MULTIPLE (assumed), not the earnings/DCF base (verifiable, ~converges). Surface and re-derive the multiple; never adopt it from price or another analyst's conclusion (Lessons 2-3).

risks.title: Power & natural-gas price normalization on the ~35% open 2028 book
risks.description: ~35% of 2028 generation is open to spot (hedge ratios 98/89/65% for 2026/27/28, S1). EIA modeling shows PJM power upside ~+4% even in a high-demand scenario; ERCOT upside is high-demand-scenario-only. A 15-20% reprice of the open book cuts 2028 EBITDA ~$0.6-0.9B (modal) to $1.4-2.3B (tail).
risks.mitigants: 98%/89% hedged 2026/27; retail integration; AWS/Meta 20-yr PPAs; ERCOT reserve margin tightening to negative by 2028 (upside)

risks.title: Hedge-collateral / margin liquidity tail (the load-bearing risk)
risks.description: Gross derivative liability peaked $11.6B (2.4x equity, Q2'22); a ~$1.9B cash margin call hit in 6 months during the 2022 gas spike. FY25 contingent collateral exposure $963M (rose 2.5x YoY). A 2x forward move could demand $3-4B vs ~$2.8B immediate dry powder (cash $785M + undrawn corporate revolver $2.0B); the dedicated $1,750M commodity-margin revolver was ~exhausted at FY25.
risks.mitigants: IG upgrades triggered collateral release; $9.2B LC/revolver facilities; demonstrated $6.25B term issuance in <4 months; covenant tested only when revolver >30% utilized (2.78x vs 4.25x first-lien)

risks.title: FERC co-location / interconnection reversal
risks.description: FERC's Dec-18-2025 order (193 FERC para 61,217, EL25-49) blesses front-of-meter contract-demand co-location and restricts pure behind-the-meter. A rehearing or D.C. Circuit reversal re-imposing full network/capacity cost on FTM co-located load would re-price co-location economics sector-wide (-15% to -30% EBITDA severe branch).
risks.mitigants: VST's Meta deal is FTM (not BTM); AWS sits in ERCOT outside FERC RTO jurisdiction; VST avoided Talen's rejected BTM structure

risks.title: PJM capacity-price reversion post cap/collar expiry
risks.description: PJM cleared at the FERC cap ($333.44/MW-day UCAP) two auctions; capacity is ~55% of East EBITDA. The cap/collar settlement expires after 2029/30; a revert toward the $179.55 floor cuts capacity revenue ~$0.6B. The 2028/29 BRA (~July 2026) is the next test.
risks.mitigants: 27/28 cleared 6,623 MW short of reliability -> uncapped equilibrium likely above the cap, so collar binds near cap through 2029/30

risks.title: 45U nuclear PTC fade / sunset (anti-double-count)
risks.description: The ~$545M 45U PTC (~9% of EBITDA) self-erases as gross receipts exceed ~$43.75/MWh (ERCOT base already $47.39) and sunsets hard end-2031/2032 under OBBBA. The bull case cannot hold both peak power prices AND full PTC; ~$15/share of EV, ~5-7% NPV steps to zero.
risks.mitigants: Largely offset in high-price scenarios by merchant uplift; transferability/direct-pay preserved through 2031

risks.title: Acquisition treadmill + thin equity / integration risk
risks.description: Energy Harbor + Lotus + Cogentrix lifted gross debt to ~$20B against thin equity ($5.1B, 12% of assets). Cogentrix pro-forma leverage ~2.85-3.0x at close (the $0.9B equity slug keeps it under 3.0x); ROIC on acquisitions and integration are watch items.
risks.mitigants: Crossover IG (S&P + Fitch BBB-); 2027 debt wall ($3.4B) being refinanced (Apr'26 $4.0B issue); FCFbG ~3x covers dividend + buyback + growth capex

risks.title: Nuclear forced-outage concentration
risks.description: ~6.4 GW nuclear across 5 sites carries the highest-margin contracted PPAs; a single large-unit forced outage is a material EBITDA hit and could impair PPA delivery.
risks.mitigants: Strong operating record; decommissioning trust over-funded ~148%; PPA backup structures (AWS customer-supplied 1:1)

risks.title: Crowded-long unwind / counterparty concentration
risks.description: Well-owned/crowded long mid-unwind (SI ~4% float rising; -34% off 2024 high; PTs trimmed though estimates intact). Meta ~69% / AWS ~31% PPA-book concentration is a single-name tail.
risks.mitigants: No squeeze (DTC ~2.3, cheap borrow); ~25-26% passive ownership + buyback absorb sellers; both counterparties top-decile IG; estimates rising not falling

catalysts.title: PJM 2028/29 Base Residual Auction clears
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Clear <$250/MW-day (vs $333 cap) confirms the bear capacity-reversion case; >$300 supports the bull.

catalysts.title: VST Q2 FY26 earnings
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: 2027 Adj EBITDA guide <$7.0B OR margin-posting disclosure >$2.8B/6mo triggers re-rate; a raise + Cogentrix-accretive 2027 supports Buy. Implied move ~+/-6-8%.

catalysts.title: Cogentrix 5.5 GW acquisition close
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: On-close, the only catalyst that mechanically lifts consensus (excluded from guidance); pro-forma leverage >3.2x would be a negative.

catalysts.title: Additional hyperscaler PPA (Comanche Unit 2 / gas co-location)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A >$100/MWh 15yr+ PPA on un-signed MW is the dominant bullish reverse trigger; continued silence keeps the optionality un-priced.

catalysts.title: FERC EL25-49 co-location compliance order
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A rehearing/reversal re-imposing cost on FTM co-located load is the severe regulatory tail; affirmation is a tailwind.

catalysts.title: AWS Comanche Peak deliveries begin
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Slippage past Q4'27 defers ~8-10% FCFbG accretion.

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: PJM 2028/29 BRA clears >$300/MW-day AND 2027 Adj EBITDA guide >=$7.6B with Cogentrix closed
what_would_reverse.observable_via: PJM 2028/29 BRA report + VST Q2/Q3 FY26 guidance

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: Comanche Peak Unit 2 (~1,200 MW) OR a gas co-location facility signs a 15-year+ PPA at >$100/MWh
2027 Adj EBITDA guidance <$7.0B (below the $7.4-7.8B opportunity) OR net leverage >3.2x post-Cogentrix
Commodity-margin posting >$2.8B in any 6-month window (exceeds dry powder; the 2022 analog was $1.89B)
Any top-4 hyperscaler cuts 2026/27 data-center capex >10% OR an AWS/Meta PPA is renegotiated/delayed past 2027 commencement
ERCOT North Hub 2028 forward settles >$55/MWh sustained (open-book uplift, net of PTC fade)

tail_risks.name: Hedge-collateral margin liquidity event (2x forward move -> $3-4B call vs ~$2.8B dry powder; 2022 analog)
FERC co-location order reversal (rehearing / D.C. Circuit) re-pricing FTM co-located load
Hyperscaler AI-capex cut / PPA renegotiation (the dominant correlated narrative tail)
ERCOT high-demand scenario + new PPA conversions (open-book + capacity uplift)

asc_606_red_flags.flag: ASC 815 unrealized hedge mark-to-market dominates GAAP earnings (not a manipulation flag, an interpretation flag)
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: FY25 GAAP NI $944M depressed by ~$808M unrealized hedge loss; Q1'26 GAAP NI $1,029M boosted by ~$723M unrealized gain. Adj EBITDA was 6.3x GAAP NI in FY25. Underwrite on Adj EBITDA / FCFbG.
Record FY25 retail EBITDA $1,622M guided down to ~$1.4B medium-term (-14%) as Energy Harbor + supply-cost tailwinds fade.

forensic_flags.vie_exposure: Vistra Vision (Energy Harbor sellers' ~15% NCI); nuclear decommissioning trusts (over-funded ~148% vs ARO)

antitrust_open_matters.matter: Cogentrix acquisition FERC Section 203 + HSR review (US buyer/US seller)

sector_regulator_open_matters.matter: Co-location / large-load interconnection framework (193 FERC para 61,217, Docket EL25-49); FTM blessed, BTM restricted; compliance pending
Operating licenses / uprates for Comanche Peak, Beaver Valley, Davis-Besse, Perry (Meta uprates)
GHG/MATS/CCR rules driving ~4.6 GW coal retirement 2027-2028; deregulatory pivot (Endangerment Finding rescinded Feb 2026) removes the forced-retirement stick
PJM capacity cap/collar (expires after 2029/30); ERCOT SB6 large-load interconnection rulemaking ($50k/MW fee)

kelly_math.conviction_multiplier_band: 0.10-0.20 (source-conditional Hold; high beta 1.41; load-bearing variance is S1/gov but two-tailed)

long_only_large_cap.mandate_constraint: Hold at ~benchmark (S&P 500 VST weight ~0.1%); high-beta utility, source-conditional; add only on a sub-$120 entry or a >$300 BRA print

long_only_smid.mandate_constraint: Hold/modest; SMID power mandates may prefer TLN for higher-beta nuclear-pure exposure or CEG for quality

long_short_hedge_fund.mandate_constraint: Express as small long-VST / short-CEG market-neutral pair (the relative-value edge), NOT a directional long; do not press a VST short into rising estimates

sector_specialty.mandate_constraint: Hold near benchmark; overweight only if BRA >$300 or a new >$100/MWh PPA signs

pair_trade.rationale: Long VST / short CEG on ~2-turn EV/EBITDA convergence + VST's +340bps FCF-yield carry. BUT the spread has already largely converged (CEG -16/-25% YTD vs VST -3/-4%) and CEG's balance sheet is cleaner (~1.7x vs 2.78x), so size SMALL and beta-hedge (~0.88); stop if the gap exceeds ~2.7 turns on a CEG hyperscaler-nuclear headline.

edge_decay.primary_decay_driver: PJM/ERCOT forward power + capacity-auction prints + hyperscaler capex + VST quarterly print

consensus_variance.narrative: Street's 2027-2028 EBITDA implicitly marks the ~35% open 2028 generation book at/above EIA STEO base and extrapolates cap-level capacity revenue. EIA modeling shows PJM upside ~+4% even in a high-demand scenario and ERCOT upside is high-demand-scenario-only; the cap/collar expires after 2029/30. Our variance shifts ~10pp of probability from base/bull into the bear tails (open book reprices -15-20%, capacity reverts toward floor), implying ~-$0.6 to -$0.9B vs a peak-extrapolated 2028. Two-tailed (ERCOT data-center load is a genuine upside), hence Hold not Sell.

evidence_refs.ref: VST hedge ratios 98/89/65% for 2026/27/28 (10-Q / IR) -> ~35% of 2028 open
EIA STEO power-price scenarios: PJM high-demand upside ~+4%; ERCOT spike high-demand-only; no published 2028 price
PJM cap/collar settlement expires after 2029/30 (FERC filing)

revision_velocity.breadth_note: PT actions ~1.3-1.5:1 up vs down; estimate breadth positive; one upgrade (Jefferies Hold->Buy 2/10), zero downgrades 3mo.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: PJM/ERCOT capacity reverts toward cost-of-new-entry floor, ~42% open 2028 book reprices down on EIA-flat demand, 45U nuclear PTC fades at higher power prices, retail normalizes to ~$1.3B; market re-rates VST to a no-growth merchant analog.
scenarios.narrative_long: Capacity auctions clear well below the 2026-27 record print as new gas/storage enters; 2028 open-book repricing lands at the low end (A-Consensus downward variance realized at ~-$0.9B, the P~60% miss compounding). PTC monetization erodes as ERCOT/PJM power prices rise above the phase-out band. Retail EBITDA mean-reverts. Multiple compresses to ~9x — the private-market gas M&A / no-growth-merchant analog — as the AI-power premium deflates.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: VST 5y avg P/E ~15x; trough merchant analog. Bear 9x = below VST 5y trough and at the private-market gas M&A multiple (Cogentrix 7.25x EV/EBITDA ~ 9-10x P/E equity). Reflects de-rating to no-growth merchant.

eps_bridge.label: 2028 open-book repricing (A-Consensus downward variance, ~-$0.9B EBITDA at low end)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: EIA PJM/ERCOT demand + Q1'26 hedge disclosure (58% 2028 hedged)

eps_bridge.label: Capacity auction reversion toward CONE floor
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: PJM/ERCOT auction sensitivity, R-bear deck

eps_bridge.label: 45U nuclear PTC fade at higher power prices (~$545M ~9% EBITDA)

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Soft landing for the bear: open-book reprices modestly down, capacity holds above floor but off peak, PPA conversions slip, retail normalizes; VST trades as a leveraged merchant with limited re-rating.
scenarios.narrative_long: The A-Consensus downward variance partially realizes (~-$0.6B, mid of the -0.6/-0.9 range). Capacity stays above CONE but well below the record. Comanche Unit 2 / gas co-location optionality is delayed rather than dead. Multiple settles at ~11x — above the trough analog but below VST's own forward (~13.8x) — pricing in cyclicality and IG-crossover credit (Moody's Ba1).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 11x = midpoint between private-market floor (~9-10x P/E equiv) and VST forward (~13.8x). Consistent with leveraged-merchant peers (NRG ~9x EV/EBITDA) before AI premium.

eps_bridge.label: 2028 open-book repricing mid-case (~-$0.6B EBITDA)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: EIA demand + Q1'26 hedge book

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: 2027 Adj EBITDA lands ~$7.6B consensus + Cogentrix accretion; hedge book (89% 2027) protects, PPAs ramp, capacity durable above floor; VST holds its forward ~14x P/E.
scenarios.narrative_long: Guidance opportunity ($7.4-7.8B) is met, Cogentrix closes 2H26 and adds mid-single-digit FCF/sh accretion in 2027. AWS+Meta 3,809 MW PPAs ramp on schedule. Open-book risk is real but offset by gas co-location and ERCOT high-demand. Stock holds ~14x — modestly above current forward 13.8x as the cash-return story (FCF yield ~7.2%, highest in peer set) is recognized.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: VST forward P/E ~13.8x (stockanalysis 6/8/26); 14x base = essentially in line, between 5y avg ~15x and current. Peer CEG ~22x fwd P/E is the re-rate ceiling, not base.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: PPA conversions land (Comanche Unit 2 + gas co-location), capacity durability proven across two auction cycles, EBITDA tops guidance toward $7.8B+; VST partially closes the gap to CEG on a re-rate to ~16.5x.
scenarios.narrative_long: AI-power demand sustains capacity prices; VST signs incremental data-center PPAs converting open-book MW to contracted premium revenue. Cogentrix accretion runs at the high single-digit end. The market begins to credit VST's nuclear+gas+retail integration with a CEG-adjacent multiple, re-rating to ~16.5x (still a ~5-turn discount to CEG's ~22x fwd P/E).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 16.5x = re-rate toward CEG (~22x fwd) but retaining a discount for merchant/leverage. Above VST 5y avg (~15x) and 2025 elevated prints; below CEG/TLN nuclear-pure multiples.

eps_bridge.label: Comanche Unit 2 + gas co-location PPA conversion (open-book -> contracted premium)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: VST IR PPA pipeline commentary

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: AI-power supercycle: multiple new hyperscaler PPAs at premium, capacity prices ratchet higher across PJM/ERCOT, full Cogentrix synergy + buyback compounding; VST re-rates to a CEG-adjacent ~18.5x.
scenarios.narrative_long: Structural power-demand shortage drives a multi-year capacity bull market; VST converts a large share of open-book to long-dated contracted PPAs, becomes an investment-grade compounder, and the FCF yield funds an aggressive buyback shrinking the share count. Multiple approaches the low end of the nuclear-pure-play band (~18.5x, still below CEG ~22x).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 18.5x approaches CEG-adjacent (~22x fwd P/E) territory; tail re-rate. Above VST's own historical band; predicated on durable contracted-nuclear/gas premium being recognized.

eps_bridge.label: Multiple incremental hyperscaler PPAs at premium
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: hyperscaler datacenter demand pipeline (AWS/Meta precedent)

eps_bridge.label: Buyback compounding on highest-in-peer FCF yield (~7.2%)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: VST capital allocation / FCF yield disclosure

headline.headline_text: VST at $146.9: de-biased 12-month expected return -14.2% (Sell/Underweight) once the base is anchored to independent fair value (~$125, -15%) instead of the current ~14x multiple; range -52.5% to +48%. Far below the stale Street PT; AI-power timing tail caps it at Sell, not Strong Sell.

consensus_variance.narrative: Street's 2027-2028 EBITDA implicitly marks the ~35% open 2028 generation book at/above EIA STEO base and extrapolates cap-level capacity revenue. EIA modeling shows PJM upside ~+4% even in a high-demand scenario and ERCOT upside is high-demand-scenario-only; the cap/collar expires after 2029/30. Our variance shifts ~10pp of probability from base/bull into the bear tails (open book reprices -15-20%, capacity reverts toward floor), implying ~-$0.6 to -$0.9B vs a peak-extrapolated 2028. Two-tailed (ERCOT data-center load is a genuine upside), hence Hold not Sell.

memo_metadata.net_leverage_note: FY25 net leverage 2.78x is a CALCULATED metric (net debt / Ongoing Adj EBITDA); not company-reported in the press release. Independent verification could not corroborate from a public source - footnoted accordingly. [v2 DE-BIASED: base anchored to independent fair value (~$125) not spot; rating Sell/Underweight; v1 Hold archived.]

adjudication_trail.attacker_dimension: AI-power demand is sticky; the open 2028 book + PPA optionality could keep VST's premium multiple for 12+ months.
adjudication_trail.attacker_rationale: PJM capacity cleared at the cap, hyperscaler PPAs are signing, and the open book is upside not just downside; the premium can persist through the horizon.
adjudication_trail.defender_rebuttal: Granted on timing - hence Sell/Underweight not Strong Sell. But VST's OWN private-market mark (Cogentrix ~7.25x) sits below the public multiple, the DCF ($107) and SOTP ($132) are both well below spot, and PJM capacity-at-the-cap is definitionally peak - so independent fair value (~$125) is below spot regardless of the open-book call.
adjudication_trail.modification_note: Survived load-bearing; sized Sell/Underweight + relative short, cover on a >$300 BRA or sub-$120 entry.

valuation_parallel.method: Two-value parallel: independent fair value (B) || market-regime value (A); the GAP is the mispricing signal; the 12-month base PT is a convergence-weighted blend.
valuation_parallel.independent_fair_value_basis: DCF $107 + SOTP $132 + de-rated comps $130 (through-cycle / private-market gas band) — computed WITHOUT reference to price (answers 'what is it worth')
valuation_parallel.market_regime_value_basis: consensus 12-mo PT median/avg (S4) — embeds the current-multiple regime (answers 'where will it trade given the regime')
valuation_parallel.convergence_assumption: base set to fair value (fast convergence assumed via PJM BRA prints + quarterly guides); de-rate to private-market multiple is the catalyst path

is_cyclical_note: Cyclical: base valued on normalized/mid-cycle earnings + P/B cross-check, never peak x forward multiple (Lesson 7).

conviction_drivers_for: independent fair value vs spot gap is the load-bearing signal (public)
DCF/SOTP/comps triangulate the fair value without reference to price
conviction_drivers_against: the multiple is a judgment that could re-rate
convergence timing is uncertain (overvaluation/undervaluation is not itself a catalyst)
conviction_axis.self_anchoring_check: BIDIRECTIONAL — the anchoring discipline applies to revisions toward EITHER side; use external analysts' PUBLIC evidence, never their conclusions (multiple/PT/rating).
conviction_axis.downgradeable_note: Conviction is tracked separately from the rating; new PUBLIC counter-evidence lowers conviction even when it does not flip the rating. A nonpublic cross-check may nuance conviction personally but is NOT persisted.
